title: A.P. farmers’ bodies to hold State-wide protests on July 22 against proposed India-US Trade Pact
content: They alleged that the pact will adversely affect Indian agriculture, depress crop prices, and threaten farmers’ livelihoods

title: Who is Anil Menon, the Indian-origin NASA astronaut of Malayali descent set to travel to space?
content: Anil Menon, NASA’s first astronaut of Malayali origin, embarks on a historic ISS mission, conducting groundbreaking research in space.

title: Government summons Iran envoy after Indian national killed in attack on ship
content: External Affairs Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said the Indian side conveyed “deepest concerns” about the incident in the Strait of Hormuz and urged for “de-escalation”

title: How Technopark in Thiruvananthapuram is soaking in the spirit of FIFA World Cup 2026
content: Companies in Technopark have organised an array of events such as Jersey Day, penalty shootout, fans’ tournament, prediction contests, and quiz ever since the tournament began

title: Contractual appointment of ex-servicemen as gatemen at level crossings irks many
content: The Railway Board has approved the appointment of around 7,220 ex-servicemen on a contractual basis for the post of gateman in the Engineering department

title: Government to monitor Indian sailors’ status in real-time as Hormuz conflict claims more lives
content: This follows attacks on the merchant vessels MT Al Bahiyah and MT Mombasa in the Strait of Hormuz. The two ships together had 30 Indian crew members in a total strength of 46

title: Incentives, warnings and assurances: how an Indian sailor transited Hormuz
content: Indian seafarer aboard oil tanker recalls incentives from his employer to make the risky transit via route near Oman, the threats from the Iranian side to stop, and encouragement from the U.S. Navy to move ahead

title: A.P. government removes Pulladigunta lands from prohibited list
content: The move paves the way for 201 families to secure full ownership rights of their lands after nearly a century; however, retains vacant government land and public institution sites under government control

title: Chief warder, two warders arrested for the death of a remand prisoner in Nagercoil jail
content: Sabari Varman, 34, a differently abled resident of Eeththankaadu near Suchindram, was arrested on July 9 for allegedly possessing the banned gutka and remanded in custody.  He died in the prison  there under suspicious circumstances on July 12

title: 18th International Documentary and Short Film Festival of Kerala to be held from September 25 to 30 in Thiruvananthapuram
content: As many as 1,741 entries have been received for the festival. The process of selecting documentaries and short films for the competition, non-competition and Malayalam non-competition categories is in progress

title: Maharashtra ATS questions 66 youth to uncover Pakistani gangster’s alleged ‘network in India’
content: Sleeper cells being formed by luring economically weaker youth via social media, says anti-terror agency

title: U.S. national held at Nepal border giving contradictory accounts, say officials
content: No passport or identity document was found on him and police recovered two mobile phones that appeared to have been ‘intentionally damaged’, a govt. official said

title: Museum housing rare palm-leaf manuscripts gets ₹5 crore funding boost
content: The Ministry of Culture has released ₹5 crore under the Museum Grant Scheme for the construction of new building of the museum - a home to nearly 5,000 palm-leaf scripts dating back to the 17th, 18th and 19th century

title: Bengaluru restaurants can remain open till 3.30 a.m. for FIFA football world cup matches
content: The order has been issued by Bengaluru City Police Commissioner Seemant Kumar Singh following a representation from the National Restaurant Association of India (NRAI)

title: Pakistan’s “decades-long exploitation” behind PoJK protests: MEA
content: “We expect and hope that the international community will hold Pakistan fully accountable for egregious abuses and misdeeds,” says MEA spokesperson

title: Old footage showing theft of donations at Gujarat’s Ambaji temple surfaces
content: A temple employee allegedly removed a bundle of cash while counting was underway; police arrested three employees

title: ED arrests Ebix group chairman Vikas Garg in illegal betting case
content: The 53-year-old businessman is alleged by the agency to have routed ill-gotten money from the betting app into the entities owned and controlled by him; the ED recorded that it has attached properties of Mr. Garg, his family members and firms owned by him, valued at ₹940.77 crore

title: Develop Mamnoor, Adilabad airports to international standards, CM urges Centre
content: Revanth Reddy requests Union Civil Aviation Minister K. Ramamohan Naidu to examine the possibility of operating seaplane services between Prakasam Barrage in Vijayawada and Hussainsagar;  no link between politics and development - politics only during elections, says Chief Minister Not appropriate to criticise the State for consulting Centre to resolve problems and speed up development

title: Passport is a document meant to ‘regulate the departure from India of citizens of India’: MEA spokesperson
content: Less than 8% of Indian citizens hold a passport, says Randhir Jaiswal; the response came weeks after a government official referred to the Indian passport as a “travel document” and said that it is not a “citizenship document”.

title: No rift in NCP, Sachchidanand Singh's letter carries no weight: Praful Patel
content: The letter written by Sachchidanand Singh carries no weight. Any issue should be resolved amicably in the interest of the party, says NCP national working president Praful Patel

title: Amended RPwD Act to include persons internally injured by acid ingestion, Centre tells Supreme Court
content: The 2016 law had previously recognised only victims of acid-throwing and not forcible acid ingestion; the amendment will have a retrospective effect

title: Pragmatic, community-inclusive approach needed to avoid land acquisition delays, says Minister
content: Minister  inaugurates curtain-raiser ceremony of the 50th anniversary of the KSCSTE - National Transportation Planning and Research Centre

title: Assembly panel pitches for Singapore-style makeover of Visakhapatnam zoo
content: Speaker flags funding gap, 59 vacancies; panel to recommend action plan for wildlife protection, legal reforms if needed

title: Veteran Kapu leader Mudragada Padmanabham passes away at 73
content: An astute politician, Padmanabham had led Andhra Pradesh’s longest social movement to restore the Backward Class status for Kapus

title: Jacobite Church reiterates call for dialogue, seeks end to litigation
content: Mor Theophilose to be installed as first Metropolitan for new Thiruvananthapuram region on Wednesday (July 15, 2026)

title: Draft national health research policy proposes overhaul of India’s research ecosystem
content: Prepared by the Department of Health Research, the draft marks the first attempt at a unified national framework spanning biomedical science, clinical medicine, public health, epidemiology, digital health, health systems, behavioural sciences and emerging technologies.

title: Naidu sets 15% GSDP growth target, unveils data-driven governance roadmap
content: Chief Minister says Monthly Economic Reports will strengthen evidence-based policymaking and accountability; asks officials to clear grievance backlog, intensify field visits and accelerate infrastructure and industrial development

title: Maharashtra government duped women under Ladki Bahin scheme: Congress
content: Congress cites the CAG report, alleging the scheme was rolled out despite warnings from the State finance department about its sustainability

title: Foundation stone for Indiramma houses in CURE on July 20
content: Ministers to announce guidelines, eligibility criteria, tenders process, implementation and other details

title: HC directs DGP to ascertain if Cherlapally police station was being run from illegally occupied building
content: The judge noted the rental agreement the Society had with the building owner had expired in 2024 and as per ninth condition of the agreement, the Society was barred from sub-leasing the premises to others without the consent of the building owner

title: ‘Donate a Memory’: Bengaluru-based Nightingales Medical Trust seeks vintage household items for India’s first integrated dementia care village
content: The Trust said familiar objects from earlier decades can serve as powerful memory cues for people with dementia, helping stimulate recognition, encourage conversations, reduce anxiety and improve emotional well-being.

title: The probe into signal failure in the Ambattur-Avadi section exposes violation of safety rules
content: Documents accessed by The Hindu reveal that the automatic signal, S192, malfunctioned over 50 times in February 2026 alone. A buzzer installed in the Ambattur Station Master’s office to alert the staff to signal failures was found to have been “disconnected”. The defective signal was repeatedly reset by field technical workers without analysing the root cause of the malfunction

title: A.P. approves ₹1,638.52 crore for phase-II expansion of three greenfield ports
content: A.P. government has approved administrative sanctions for land acquisition to facilitate the second phase of expansion of Ramayapatnam, Mulapeta and Machilipatnam ports

title: Indian Kanoon challenges Delhi HC ruling recognising ‘right to be forgotten’, says it undermines open justice
content: The appeal challenged the single-judge judgment that allowed individuals to seek removal of personal information from search engine results, including legal databases, where such information is ‘no longer relevant or serves no legitimate public purpose’

title: Kerala Minister Bindhu Krishna says photos of her body circulated online
content: Minister says she was shocked that such a photograph could be taken while she was attending an official programme with students and teachers

title: Watch: Russia strikes Ukraine with a 3,000 kg bomb amid escalating conflict
content: Russia released footage of a massive FAB-3000 glide bomb strike in Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia region. European nations announced an air-defence coalition to develop a ballistic missile defence system. Ukrainian forces intensified drone and maritime attacks deep inside Russian territory. Russia vowed retaliation against future Ukrainian strikes, escalating tensions further. France celebrated its national day, showcasing European military strength and support for Ukraine.

title: Indian-origin astronaut Anil Menon lifts-off to ISS: Inside Russia's eight-month mission
content: Nasa astronaut Anil Menon launched for his first spaceflight mission. He will spend eight months aboard the International Space Station. Menon will conduct scientific experiments and technology demonstrations. His research focuses on human adaptation to microgravity and medical advancements. This mission continues cooperation between Nasa and Roscosmos despite global tensions.

title: Meta layoffs: 26 ex-employees sue, allege medical info was used to target them
content: Former Meta employees filed a federal lawsuit alleging AI-driven discrimination during recent mass layoffs. The suit claims the company's internal system unfairly targeted vulnerable workers for termination. These workers argue automated performance metrics disadvantaged those with medical conditions. Meta denies these claims, stating human decisions, not AI, guided the workforce changes. CEO Mark Zuckerberg admitted executives miscalculated the restructuring's timing and impact.

title: Injury scare for India: Shubman Gill walks off retired hurt in England ODI
content: The injury came just after Gill and Shreyas Iyer had added a 100-run partnership to put India in control of the chase. Washington Sundar came in as the replacement batter.

title: Jeff Bezos’ wife Lauren Sánchez leads $100M investment to transform 8 US cities
content: Lauren Sanchez Bezos is directing significant Bezos Earth Fund investments. The fund is transforming urban spaces into green community hubs. This initiative aims to connect people and nature within cities. The Bezos Earth Fund also supports marine protection and AI climate solutions. Overall, the fund continues its commitment to climate and nature initiatives.

title: From $120m monastery to Miami home: Alex Karp’s property portfolio
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp has amassed a significant global real estate portfolio.  He owns a 500-acre estate in rural New Hampshire for privacy.  Karp also purchased two adjacent waterfront Miami properties for a compound.  His portfolio includes a $120 million former monastery near Aspen.  These properties reflect his desire for solitude and personal space.

title: Skip bathtub curtains, this vintage trick can make bathrooms look expensive
content: Folding screens offer a vintage alternative to shower curtains for bathrooms.  These decorative pieces originated in China and were used as room dividers.  You can find affordable second-hand screens at thrift stores and flea markets.  Repurposing old room dividers or building your own is also an option.  Protecting wooden screens with sealant is essential in humid bathroom environments.

title: IBM stock sees worst in 39 years, CEO admits company 'failed' to close large deals
content: IBM's stock experienced a historic collapse on Wall Street after missing financial expectations.  Preliminary second-quarter revenue fell short as clients shifted spending towards hardware.  Chief Executive Arvind Krishna acknowledged the company's failure to adapt quickly enough.  Internal teams faltered, causing numerous large deals to miss expected timelines.  This shift benefited hardware manufacturers while impacting software providers.

title: Watch: Russia successfully launches Indian-origin Nasa astronaut Anil Menon to space
content: Astronaut Anil Menon, an Indian-American, has launched to the International Space Station. He is traveling aboard Russia's Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft from Kazakhstan. Menon will spend eight months conducting scientific research on the orbiting laboratory. This mission marks his very first journey into space. He joins other international astronauts already aboard the station.

title: Cheaper cars & whisky: India-UK trade effective from July 15 - How India will benefit
content: With its implementation, the trade deal will become the sixth free trade agreement brought into force during the Narendra Modi government's tenure. India has previously implemented similar agreements with Mauritius, the UAE, Australia, the European Free Trade Association (EFTA) and Oman.

title: Messi's 'lucky referee'? FIFA appointment for England vs Argentina sparks fresh debate
content: Moroccan-American referee Ismail Elfath will officiate the World Cup semi-final between England and Argentina. Elfath has previously overseen several significant matches involving Lionel Messi. Online speculation has arisen due to these past coincidences and Messi's success in those games. FIFA has defended its referees against claims of favoritism towards Argentina. Elfath's disciplinary record suggests a tightly controlled contest for both teams.

title: How Jude Bellingham's parents shaped England's football superstar
content: Denise and Mark Bellingham have been central to Jude Bellingham’s rise from a young footballer in Birmingham to one of England’s biggest stars. Through career sacrifices, constant support, and important decisions, they have helped shape both Jude and Jobe’s careers. While reports have questioned their relationship, they continue to stand behind their sons’ success.

title: Tom Segura, Christina P split after 18 years, will continue podcast together
content: Tom Segura and Christina P have reportedly separated after 18 years of marriage, according to TMZ. The news has surprised many fans who have followed the couple’s journey through comedy, podcasts and television projects. While the pair have remained largely private about the situation, reports suggest they will continue working together, leaving plenty of questions still unanswered.

title: Bruins, Flyers eye $2.6m Kraken star Shane Wright in trade buzz
content: The Seattle Kraken’s star player Shane Wright ended the last season with 12 goals and 27 points in 74 games, sparking concern about him. It seems this has led him to actively seek trade from the team as the Seattle kraken is reportedly actively working to move him. Amid this, the Boston Bruins and the Philadelphia Flyers have been linked to him.

title: 1.44kg gold, 12kg silver: Raid on Hyderabad civic official uncovers Rs 9.24cr assets
content: Telangana ACB registered a case against HMDA Chief Engineer B Ravinder. Searches unearthed assets valued at over Rs 9.24 crore from his properties. Officials recovered cash, bank deposits, and significant gold and silver ornaments. Documents revealed ownership of plots, flats, agricultural land, and buildings. The investigation into further assets is currently ongoing.

title: Trump's Section 301 probe: Why India's forced labour import ban matters
content: The move comes against the backdrop of a US investigation into forced labour practices across 60 economies, including India. The notification said the new provisions will take effect after the completion of 30 days from the date of their publication in the Official Gazette.

title: 'No rift in party': NCP rejects challenge to Sunetra Pawar's election as chief
content: NCP leaders Praful Patel and Sunil Tatkare dismissed a notice questioning Sunetra Pawar's election. They asserted the election was constitutional and the notice held no significance. The notice alleged Sunetra Pawar's election was unconstitutional and should be declared void. Tatkare called the notice illegal and stated the party would examine its legal validity. The party remains united under Sunetra Pawar's leadership, according to leaders.

title: 'Systemic exploitation': India urges world to hold Pak 'fully accountable' over PoK unrest
content: India stated Pakistan's exploitation causes protests in Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir. The ministry of external affairs spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal accused Islamabad of excessive force against public dissent. India maintains Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir is an integral part of India. New Delhi also accused Pakistan of human rights violations in the region.

title: Institutional investments in Indian real estate jump 58% in Jan-June: Report
content: In the first half of 2026, India’s real estate sector saw unprecedented institutional investments totaling $4.1 billion. This remarkable growth was driven by both heightened domestic capital involvement and an influx of foreign investors. The commercial real estate sector garnered the majority of funding, particularly due to the presence of Global Capability Centres. Strong investment trends are anticipated to continue, as market uncertainties gradually diminish.

title: WC semifinal preview: France vs Spain - can Mbappe and Co be stopped?
content: On Bastille Day, Mbappé’s rampant France face the Bourbon kingdom that has stopped them twice, with Yamal and Spain’s iron defence standing between Les Bleus and a third successive World Cup final.

title: MS Dhoni and Kriti Sanon steal the spotlight at India vs England ODI
content: MS Dhoni and Kriti Sanon attended the India versus England ODI match. England collapsed from a strong start to a low total. Jasprit Bumrah and Gurnoor Brar took key wickets. Joe Root and Liam Dawson led a spirited recovery for England. Axar Patel's late spell restricted England to 258 runs.

title: 'They chased us for 15 km': Daughter recounts assault on mother; 9 arrested in Nashik
content: What began as a peaceful family picnic near Bhavali Dam quickly escalated into a nightmare. Two young men harassed a woman, prompting her father to intervene. This led to a violent chase, where the family's SUV was followed and attacked by a gang on motorcycles. Allegations of molestation and assault surfaced, prompting police action that resulted in nine arrests after the family provided video evidence.

title: OBC status of 77 castes: Suvendu govt withdraws Mamata-era SC challenge to HC verdict
content: West Bengal withdrew its Supreme Court appeal challenging the High Court's OBC list judgment. The state cabinet decided not to pursue the appeal, which was previously filed by the Trinamool Congress government. The Calcutta High Court had struck down the inclusion of 77 communities, including 75 Muslim communities. The state assembly has since passed two bills to restructure the OBC reservation framework.

title: 'He said he'd be back next week': Kin shares victim's final call before Vietnam boat tragedy
content: Ravi Teja's family shared his final joyful video calls before the Vietnam boat tragedy. He enthusiastically showed his family scenic spots and the boat journey. His daughter recalled his promise to return next Wednesday for a program. His mother spoke with him for thirty minutes Saturday morning. Mortal remains of Ravi Teja and other victims were repatriated.

title: Why do we forget why we walked into a room? The fascinating science
content: Entering a new room can cause temporary memory lapses for your original intention. This doorway effect is a normal brain process for organizing experiences into distinct events.  It happens as your brain updates its mental model of the environment.  Working memory limitations and attention shifts also contribute to these forgetful moments.  Retracing your steps often helps retrieve the forgotten thought by restoring context.

title: Govt releases first services production index; 14 sub-sectors post double-digit growth
content: India's government has launched a new Index of Services Production. Fourteen of nineteen formal services sub-sectors showed double-digit growth in April. Accommodation and food services expanded significantly, leading the growth figures. Air and railway transport were the only sub-sectors to record declines. This index will now be released monthly to track economic activity.

title: 'Political conspiracy': Delhi CM blames AAP top brass over Tahir Hussain conviction
content: Rekha Gupta accused the Aam Aadmi Party of political patronage to Tahir Hussain. She demanded answers from the party leadership regarding their association with him. Hussain was convicted for his role in the 2020 Delhi riots and murder. The conviction brought justice to victims and their devastated families. The chief minister stressed accountability and punishment for those responsible.

title: 13 Indians killed, 3 missing in Gulf since February 28 amid US-Iran war
content: Thirteen Indians have died and three are missing in the Gulf region since late February. Recent attacks on commercial ships have resulted in significant Indian casualties. India is closely monitoring the escalating regional crisis and its impact. The nation calls for safe and uninterrupted navigation through the Strait of Hormuz. Attacks on shipping must cease to ensure global trade resumes.

title: Thane hospital assault case: Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre gets bail
content: A local court granted bail to Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre on Tuesday. He was arrested for allegedly assaulting doctors and medical staff at a civic hospital. Mhatre and three associates were sent to judicial custody following the incident. The alleged assault triggered widespread outrage across Maharashtra after a video went viral. The two doctors Mhatre is accused of assaulting have since submitted their resignations.

title: Beyond the border: How India is building firepower to strike targets 1,000km away
content: India's military is enhancing long-range strike capabilities for future conflicts. New weapons and drone technology are increasing engagement distances significantly. These advancements allow for precise strikes deep within enemy territory. India aims to control escalation and dictate conflict resolution terms. This modernization drive ensures a continued combat edge in the region.

title: All not well between Gambhir & Agarkar? Dinesh Karthik drops 'friction' bombshell
content: Dinesh Karthik suggests friction between Gautam Gambhir and Ajit Agarkar over selection priorities.  India's recent T20I series losses in the UK have intensified scrutiny on the team's management.  Karthik believes differing long-term plans and immediate winning desires cause selection confusion.  The immense squad depth adds pressure on players struggling to secure their places.  The BCCI will review these poor T20I performances and selections soon.

title: World Cup Semi-Final: Why England vs Argentina is more 'war' than football
content: A World Cup semi-final brings together two nations separated by an ocean and united by six decades of grudges, ghosts and glorious bad blood.

title: Brothers detained by Pak forces found dead in Balochistan; custody death concerns raised
content: Two brothers, Imam and Muhammad Umar, were found dead with bullet injuries in Balochistan.  They were allegedly taken into custody by security personnel after their motorcycle ran out of fuel.  Their family later received their bodies, disputing claims of militant links.  Local residents expressed concern over the circumstances surrounding their deaths.  No official response has been issued by Pakistan's military or government.

title: Not ‘treasured’! Why India, China are stacking up gold & trimming US Treasuries exposure
content: India is among major economies that are stepping up purchases of gold, while trimming holdings of US Treasuries. Central banks have increasingly begun viewing gold not only as a hedge against inflation but also as a core store of value.

title: Two things my DNA told me that eleven years of blood tests never could
content: Genetic sequencing revealed unique responses to rosuvastatin medication for Nickhil Jakatdar. His body processes the statin more slowly than average, influencing future treatment decisions. A variant associated with Gilbert syndrome was also identified through his genome. This finding indicated that prolonged intermittent fasting might not be universally beneficial for him. These nuanced genetic insights provided confidence to tailor his health approach.

title: 'Had engine problem': Vlogger Sourav Joshi backtracks on E20 petrol mileage claim
content: YouTuber Sourav Joshi clarified his Mercedes-Benz SUV mileage concerns after switching to E20 petrol. Mercedes-Benz India stated all its BS VI petrol vehicles are compatible with E20 fuel. Joshi later explained an engine issue caused the low mileage, not the fuel itself. He apologized for any misunderstanding and trimmed the controversial vlog segment. The company reiterated its commitment to customer safety and sustainable mobility.

title: From Rahul to Vijay to Nadda: CJP seeks support for NEET protesters
content: The Cockroach Janta Party has invited political leaders to join a protest at Jantar Mantar. This demonstration supports students protesting the alleged NEET UG examination irregularities and paper leak. Leaders from various parties have been urged to stand in solidarity with the protesting youth. The organization seeks accountability and justice for students affected by the education system. Many leaders have already offered support, while others are still being contacted.

title: Buying a home in Delhi-NCT? Here's how to register your property
content: Proper property registration is essential to validate ownership and maintain a public record. When it comes to sale deeds, applicable stamp duty and registration fees are determined by the sale's value. Although will registration is not mandatory, it reinforces ownership claims and minimises disputes. Furthermore, registration charges apply to gift deeds and share certificates, ensuring that buyers' rights are safeguarded against potential legal challenges.

title: 'If Missile Hits Supertanker Cargo': Indian Sailor's Worst Fear In Strait Of Hormuz
content: Indian sailors who steered supertankers in the Strait of Hormuz narrate the challenges of keeping alive the complex system of transport that holds the world together, but at a great cost to their own safety and lives

title: Bangladeshi Man Detained In Delhi. He Was Living Disguised As Eunuch
content: During interrogation, the accused allegedly admitted that he used to dress as a eunuch at night and was involved in illegal activities.

title: Trump Can't Think 'Strait' On Hormuz. Drops Transit Fee, Wants Deals
content: Trump spoke about how factories, plants and equipment will "pour" into the United States at "historic" levels, which will in turn create high-paying jobs.

title: India-UK Trade Agreement To Come Into Force Tomorrow
content: Per the deal, from July 15, India's textiles, leather and footwear, gems and jewellery and plastics exports will enter the British market with zero duty.

title: This European Country Never Stopped Preparing For War. It Shows Underground
content: Finland's capital lies fewer than 200 miles from St Petersburg, and the country shares an 830-mile border with Russia, the longest that any NATO member has with Moscow.

title: Opinion: 'Zalengam Diary' And Roads As New Language In Demand To Divide Manipur
content: There have been many instances when infrastructure preceded politics. By connecting territories, roads can gradually reshape political demands and make some constitutional futures more conceivable than others

title: Until The Last Journey: Betul Man's Loyal Dog Dies Beside His Bier
content: Pradeep Jain, 67, a resident of Betul's Civil Lines area, died while undergoing treatment at AIIMS Bhopal after a prolonged illness.

title: Opposition Walks Out Of Assam Assembly Demanding Tribal Status For 6 Communities
content: Leader of the Opposition said the BJP had repeatedly promised to grant ST status to the six communities before elections but had failed to fulfil it after coming to power.

title: Indian-Origin Astronaut Anil Menon Blasts Off For Maiden Space Station Trip
content: Indian-origin astronaut Anil Menon launched from the historic Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan along with Russian cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina.

title: Army Soldier, Father Killed By Cousin In Bihar After Fight Over Banana Tree
content: The Army soldier, Jitendra Kumar, was posted in Delhi and had returned home a few days ago after a relative died from electrocution.

title: 3 Women Workers Killed, 6 Injured After Tractor Overturns In Bihar's Araria
content: The accident occurred on the western embankment of the Parihari distributary canal in Sirsia Hanumanganj panchayat.

title: Man, 63, Shoots Wife Dead, Then Tries To Kill Self In UP's Shahjahanpur
content: Additional Superintendent of Police (Rural) Diksha Bhavare said Harpal (63) argued with his wife, Laxmi (60), which escalated.

title: Cop Killed, 3 Injured After Police Van Rams Parked Truck In Jharkhand's Dhanbad
content: The incident took place around 3.30 am on GT Road in Barwaada police station area when the policemen were returning to Hazaribag after a raid in Maithan Dam outpost area in Dhanbad, police said.

title: Paramedical Student, 22, Stabbed To Death By Classmate Inside UP Hospital
content: Victim Kashish Patel of Bareilly, who was pursuing a diploma in CT-scan, sustained severe injuries on the neck and was referred to a private hospital in Bareilly.

title: 2 Terror Suspects Caught On CCTV In Jammu, Hunt Underway
content: The visuals of two suspected terrorists caught on camera prompted the Special Operations Group (SOG) of J&K Police, CRPF and the Army to launch a well-coordinated cordon and search operation.
